{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "module": "CommonJS",
    "target": "ES2022",
    "lib": ["ES2022"],
    "types": ["node", "jest"],
    "outDir": "dist",
    "rootDir": "src",
    "strict": true,
    "noImplicitAny": true,
    "strictNullChecks": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "sourceMap": true,
    "declaration": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true
  },
  "include": ["src"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist", "**/*.test.ts"]
}

{
  "version": "1.0",
  "sessions": [
    {
      "id": "manual-test-session-001",
      "source": "import",
      "startedAt": "2026-05-31T10:00:00Z",
      "endedAt": "2026-05-31T10:12:00Z",
      "messages": [
        {
          "id": "manual-test-msg-001",
          "sessionId": "manual-test-session-001",
          "role": "user",
          "content": "I am building a React and TypeScript component library. I already created a Button component, but I need to understand the tradeoffs between props, variants, and composition. Please explain the architecture and include how I should test it with Jest.",
          "timestamp": "2026-05-31T10:00:00Z",
          "tokenCount": 54,
          "estimatedCostUSD": 0.0002,
          "source": "import"
        },
        {
          "id": "manual-test-msg-002",
          "sessionId": "manual-test-session-001",
          "role": "assistant",
          "content": "Use a small typed prop surface for stable API guarantees, then use composition for advanced layout cases. For variants, define a constrained union such as primary, secondary, and danger so styling is predictable. Test rendering, click behavior, disabled behavior, and class or data-state output with Jest and React Testing Library.",
          "timestamp": "2026-05-31T10:03:00Z",
          "tokenCount": 67,
          "estimatedCostUSD": 0.0003,
          "source": "import"
        },
        {
          "id": "manual-test-msg-003",
          "sessionId": "manual-test-session-001",
          "role": "user",
          "content": "I tried a variant prop, but I am worried it will become too large. What boundary should I use before moving behavior into separate components? Also, how would I test accessibility states?",
          "timestamp": "2026-05-31T10:07:00Z",
          "tokenCount": 39,
          "estimatedCostUSD": 0.0001,
          "source": "import"
        },
        {
          "id": "manual-test-msg-004",
          "sessionId": "manual-test-session-001",
          "role": "assistant",
          "content": "Keep variants for visual intent only. When a variant starts changing structure, data flow, or interaction behavior, create a separate component or expose a slot/composition pattern. For accessibility tests, assert the accessible role, name, disabled state, focus behavior, keyboard activation, and aria attributes when applicable.",
          "timestamp": "2026-05-31T10:12:00Z",
          "tokenCount": 65,
          "estimatedCostUSD": 0.0003,
          "source": "import"
        }
      ],
      "totalInputTokens": 93,
      "totalOutputTokens": 132,
      "totalCostUSD": 0.0009,
      "status": "complete"
    }
  ]
}
